sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Olympique de Marseille, sitting in 2nd place with 59 points, host Stade Brestois, who are 9th with 47 points, in Round 31 at the iconic Orange Velodrome in Marseille. This matchup promises to be intriguing given Marseille’s strong league standing and home advantage. Stade Brestois will be looking to close the gap and secure vital points on the road. The referee for this clash, Stephanie Frappart, averages 3.7 yellow cards per game, suggesting a potentially heated encounter. With both teams having recent trends of conceding goals, as Marseille have gone 6 games without a clean sheet and Brestois 4, fans can expect an open game.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these sides, it’s an even contest with 2 wins for Marseille, 2 for Brestois, and 1 draw. Their most recent encounter on August 17, 2024, saw Marseille dominate with a 5-1 away victory, where Mason Greenwood earned Player of the Match honors with an impressive 8.5 Sofascore rating. Historically, both teams have scored in 8 of their last 10 meetings, and Marseille have been first to score in 8 of those 10 games. Head-to-head stats also show Marseille often leading at halftime, winning the first half in 7 of their last 9 encounters against Brestois.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Marseille come into this game with a streak of 5 consecutive matches featuring over 2.5 goals, showcasing their attacking prowess with 67 total goals this season, ranking 2nd in the league. However, their defense has struggled, conceding in their last 6 games. Stade Brestois also have a trend of high-scoring games, with over 2.5 goals in 4 of their last 5 matches, and they’ve failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 4 outings. Both teams scoring has occurred in 4 of the last 5 games for each side, indicating a high likelihood of goals at both ends. Marseille tend to have fewer than 10.5 corners in 7 of their last 8 games, while Brestois have seen over 10.5 corners in 5 of their last 6.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Marseille
sections.text: Mason Greenwood has been a standout for Marseille, ranking 2nd in the league with 18 goals and holding a 4th place average Sofascore rating of 7.51. His expected goals of 14.19 show he’s overperforming by 3.81, making him a constant threat. Amine Gouiri also contributes significantly with 11 goals and 5 assists, while Luís Henrique adds creativity with 7 goals and 6 assists. Midfielder Adrien Rabiot, with 7 goals and a solid 7.34 rating, will be crucial in controlling the game. Keep an eye on Pierre-Emile Højbjerg as well, whose 7.35 rating reflects his consistent impact in the midfield.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Brestois
sections.text: Stade Brestois will miss key forward Ludovic Ajorque, who has 12 goals this season, due to absence, which could hurt their attack. Abdallah Sima steps up with 7 goals, while Romain Del Castillo offers balance in midfield with 6 goals and 3 assists. Mathias Pereira Lage, in form over the last 5 matches with a 7.52 average rating and 4 assists, will be pivotal. Pierre Lees Melou, with a 7.19 rating, brings stability to the midfield and will aim to disrupt Marseille’s rhythm.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Marseille boast the league’s 2nd-best ball possession at 63.97% and rank 2nd in accurate passes at 89.76%, indicating their dominance in dictating play. They excel in the final third with 126.81 successful passes per game, also ranking 2nd, and create big chances, ranking 4th with 99 created this season. Brestois, however, are dangerous on the counter, with a notable 22.64% of goals coming in the 76-90 minute period. Marseille’s goal distribution shows strength in the 61-75 minute slot with 19 goals, suggesting late surges could decide the game. Defensively, Marseille’s 54 yellow cards (5th fewest in the league) contrast with Brestois’ tendency for over 4.5 cards in 4 of their last 5 games.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Stats
sections.text: Marseille have hit the woodwork 13 times this season, ranking 6th in unluckiest teams, which could play into their hunger to convert chances. They also rank 2nd in aerial duels won at 54.68%, potentially giving them an edge in set-piece situations. Brestois defender Massadio Haïdara ranks 5th in the league for being fouled per game at 2.2 times, which might draw fouls against Marseille’s 374 committed fouls (12th most in the league). Marseille’s goalkeeper Gerónimo Rulli has been exceptional against Brestois historically, averaging 4.67 saves per game over 3 starts with a 7.8 Sofascore rating. Finally, Marseille convert 42.42% of big chances, ranking 3rd in the league, while missing 57, ranking 12th for least missed.
